item.title: Government Appoints Donna Ockenden as Leeds Hospitals Trust Maternity Inquiry Chair
item.description: The UK government has appointed Donna Ockenden as the chair of an independent inquiry into maternity failures at Leeds Teaching Hospitals NHS Trust, following a campaign by bereaved and harmed families.

item.title: Signal Warns of Scams as Hackers Target Officials and Users
item.description: Signal has warned its users to be cautious of scams after hackers targeted high-profile users, including officials and journalists, using sophisticated phishing campaigns.

item.title: Inmate Charged with Murder of Notorious Killer Ian Huntley
item.description: A prison inmate has been charged with the murder of Ian Huntley, the former school caretaker who murdered 10-year-olds Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in 2002.

item.title: Family of Tumbler Ridge Shooting Victim Sues OpenAI
item.description: The family of Maya Gebala, a 12-year-old girl injured in a Canadian school shooting, has sued OpenAI, alleging the company failed to alert authorities to the suspect's plans.

item.title: Violence Against Women: A Growing Epidemic in the UK
item.description: A recent investigation has found that at least 90 women or girls were killed by men in the UK in 2025, with 24 resulting in convictions for murder and six for manslaughter.

item.title: Anthropic Sues US Government Over 'Supply Chain Risk' Label
item.description: Anthropic sues US government over 'supply chain risk' label, citing 'unprecedented and unlawful' action.
